sanskrit: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय । सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: हे धनञ्जय  तू आसक्तिका त्याग करके सिद्धिअसिद्धिमें सम होकर योगमें स्थित हुआ कर्मोंको कर क्योंकि समत्व ही योग कहा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: O Dhananjaya, perform actions while established in yoga, abandoning attachment. Remain equal in success and failure, for equanimity is called yoga.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Keep doing your duty without clinging to results. Stay steady whether things work out or not; that steady balance is yoga.
simpleInsight: You can stay calm without stopping action. Inner balance makes effort cleaner and less painful.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before the war. Krishna has already told him to act without craving results; now he sharpens that teaching: stay balanced in victory and defeat, because that inner evenness is the heart of yoga.
modernRelevance: You send the message, then stare at the screen waiting for a reply. The mind rises and falls with every notification; this verse teaches you to keep your centre anyway.
translationLiteral: योगस्थः (established in yoga) / कुरु (do) / कर्माणि (actions) / सङ्गं (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya) / सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः (in success and failure) / समः (equal) / भूत्वा (having become) / समत्वं (equanimity) / योगः (yoga) / उच्यते (is called)
